folio.label: 129r
folio.l: She seide Sire , Sithe of ©oure gentillesse
ñe profre me , to haue so large a reyne
Ne wolde neuäe god , bytwixte vs tweyne
As in my gilt , were either werre or strif
Sire , I wil be ©oure humble , trewe wyf
Haue here my trouthe , til that myn herte breste
Thus ben they bothe , in quiete and in reste
¢ For oo thyngö sires , safly dar I seye
That frendes , euäicú other , mot obeye
If they wiln longe , holden compaignye
Loue wol nat be constreyned , by maistrye
Whan maistrie cometú , god of loue a noon
Betetú hise wynges , and fare wel he is gon
Loue is a thyngö , as any spirit free
Wo~men of kynde , desiren libertee
And nat to be constreyned , as a thral
And so doon men , If I sootú seyn , shal
Loke who that most , is pacient in loue
He is at his auauntage , al a boue
Pacience , is an heigú vertu , certeyn
For it venquysshetú , alle theise clerkes seyn
Thynges , that rigour shulde neuäe ateyne
For euäy word , men moun nat chide or pleyne
Lernetú to suffre , or elles so mot I gon
ñe shuln it lerne , wheiæä so ©e wol or noon
For in this world certeyn , there no wygút is
That he ne dotú or seitú , somtyme amys
Ire , sykenesse , or constellaciou~
Wyn , woo , or chaungyngö of complexiouö
Causetú ful ofte , to don a mys or spekyn
On euäy wrongö , a man may nat be wrekyn
After the tyme , must be tem¹aunce
To euäy wygút , that can on gouänaunce
And therfore hatú , this wys worthy knygút
To lyue in ease , suffraunce hire behigút
And she to hi~ , ful wysly gan to swere
That neuäe shulde there be , defaute in hire
¢ Heere moun men sen , an humble wys accord
Thus hatú she take hire seruau~t , & hire lord
Seruaunt in loue , and lord in mariage
Thanne was he , bothe in lordshipe , & säuage
Seruage nay , but in lordshipe a boue
Sithe he hatú bothe , his lady and his loue
